solution: 
class Solution:
    def arrangeWords(self, text: str) -> str:
        words = text.split()
        words[0] = words[0].lower()
        words.sort(key=lambda w: len(w))
        words[0] = words[0][0].upper() + words[0][1:]
        return ' '.join(words)
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Split the sentence into words, lowercase the first word to avoid case issues, stable-sort by length, capitalize the new first word, and join back into a sentence.
